\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Uniqueness of the Hilbert polynomial]
  \label{lem:hilbertPolynomial_unique}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomial_eq_of_eventually}
  \uses{def:fiber_hilbert_function}
  % ENCODING. In Lean the uniqueness is phrased against the chosen value:
  % Scheme.hilbertPolynomial_eq_of_eventually states that ANY polynomial Φ
  % eventually agreeing with hilbertFunction π L F s equals
  % hilbertPolynomial π L F s. Its core is the private helper
  % eq_of_eval_nat_eventually_eq (two rational polynomials agreeing at all
  % large naturals are equal, via Polynomial.eq_of_infinite_eval_eq), which is
  % the statement below. The thin corollary
  % Scheme.hilbertPolynomial_eval_eventually (the defining eventual-agreement
  % property of hilbertPolynomial under the existence hypothesis) is
  % formalized in the same file and carried by this node. All sorry-free in
  % AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/HilbertPolynomial.lean.
  Let \(h_{\mathcal{F},s}\) be the graded Hilbert function of
  \cref{def:fiber_hilbert_function}. If \(\Phi, \Psi \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\)
  both agree with \(h_{\mathcal{F},s}\) at all sufficiently large integers --
  there are \(N_1, N_2 \in \mathbb{N}\) with
  \(\Phi(m) = h_{\mathcal{F},s}(m)\) for all \(m > N_1\) and
  \(\Psi(m) = h_{\mathcal{F},s}(m)\) for all \(m > N_2\) -- then
  \(\Phi = \Psi\). In particular a polynomial agreeing with
  \(h_{\mathcal{F},s}\) for all \(m \gg 0\) is unique when it exists.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \leanok
  The difference \(\Phi - \Psi \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\) vanishes at every
  integer \(m > \max(N_1, N_2)\), hence at infinitely many points of
  \(\mathbb{Q}\). A nonzero polynomial over a field has at most
  \(\deg(\Phi - \Psi)\) roots, so \(\Phi - \Psi = 0\), that is
  \(\Phi = \Psi\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Hilbert polynomial from a rational Hilbert function]
  \label{lem:hilbertPoly_of_isRatHilb}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.existsUnique_hilbertPolynomial_of_isRatHilb}
  \uses{def:fiber_hilbert_function, def:ratHilb}
  % ENCODING. Scheme.existsUnique_hilbertPolynomial_of_isRatHilb: from
  % IsRatHilb (fun m => (hilbertFunction π L F s m : ℚ)) d -- the output shape
  % of the graded Hilbert-Serre engine gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational
  % (lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational) -- an ∃! polynomial eventually agreeing
  % with the graded Hilbert function, with witness Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d
  % via Polynomial.coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval
  % (lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib). The thin corollaries
  % Scheme.hilbertPolynomial_eval_of_isRatHilb (under IsRatHilb,
  % hilbertPolynomial itself eventually agrees with the graded Hilbert
  % function) and Scheme.hilbertPolynomial_eq_hilbertPoly (under an explicit
  % rational-series presentation, hilbertPolynomial π L F s =
  % Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d) are formalized in the same file and carried by
  % this node. All sorry-free in
  % AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/HilbertPolynomial.lean.
  Let \(h_{\mathcal{F},s}\) be the graded Hilbert function of
  \cref{def:fiber_hilbert_function}, and suppose \(h_{\mathcal{F},s}\) is a
  rational Hilbert function of some order \(d\) (\cref{def:ratHilb}): there
  are \(p \in \mathbb{Q}[X]\) and \(N \in \mathbb{N}\) with
  \(h_{\mathcal{F},s}(m) = [X^m]\bigl(p \cdot (1-X)^{-d}\bigr)\) for all
  \(m > N\). Then there exists a unique polynomial
  \(\Phi \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\) such that
  \(\Phi(m) = h_{\mathcal{F},s}(m)\) for all \(m \gg 0\).
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:ratHilb, lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib,
    lem:hilbertPolynomial_unique}
  \leanok
  Unfolding \cref{def:ratHilb} supplies \(p \in \mathbb{Q}[X]\),
  \(d \in \mathbb{N}\) and \(N\) with
  \(h_{\mathcal{F},s}(m) = [X^m](p\cdot(1-X)^{-d})\) for all \(m > N\). By
  \cref{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib} over the characteristic-zero field
  \(\mathbb{Q}\), applied to the pair \((p, d)\), there are a polynomial
  \(h \in \mathbb{Q}[X]\) and an \(N'\) with
  \(h(n) = [X^n](p\cdot(1-X)^{-d})\) for all \(n > N'\). Hence
  \(\Phi := h\) satisfies \(\Phi(m) = h_{\mathcal{F},s}(m)\) for all
  \(m > \max(N, N')\), proving existence. Uniqueness is
  \cref{lem:hilbertPolynomial_unique}: any two polynomials agreeing with
  \(h_{\mathcal{F},s}\) at all sufficiently large integers are equal.
\end{proof}

\begin{theorem}[Hilbert polynomial from the section module]
  \label{thm:hilbertPoly_of_sectionModule}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomialOfSectionModule}
  \uses{def:hilbert_polynomial, def:fiber_hilbert_function}
  \source{nitsure-hilbert-quot}
  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
  % Polynomials" (read from
  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L468-L478).
  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
  Let \(X_s\) be proper over the field \(\kappa(s)\), let \(L_s\) be an ample line
  bundle on \(X_s\), and let \(\mathcal{F}_s\) be a coherent sheaf on \(X_s\).
  Then there is a unique polynomial \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\)
  such that for all \(m \gg 0\)
  \[
    \Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}(m)
    \;=\; \dim_{\kappa(s)} \Gamma\bigl(X_s,\, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}\bigr).
  \]
  This \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}\) is the Hilbert polynomial of
  \cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}.

  \emph{Status.} The polynomial-extraction half of this theorem is formalized:
  uniqueness (\cref{lem:hilbertPolynomial_unique}) and the extraction of the
  unique polynomial from a rational Hilbert function
  (\cref{lem:hilbertPoly_of_isRatHilb},
  \texttt{Scheme.existsUnique\_hilbertPolynomial\_of\_isRatHilb}) are
  sorry-free. The remaining input is the geometric finite-generation theorem
  \cref{lem:sectionGradedModule_fg} (Serre), which feeds the rationality
  hypothesis through \cref{lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational}.
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:sectionGradedModule_fg, lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational,
    lem:hilbertPoly_of_isRatHilb}
  By \cref{lem:sectionGradedModule_fg} the section graded module
  \(M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s)\) is a finitely generated graded module over the
  Noetherian graded ring \(R(X_s, L_s)\), with finite-dimensional components and
  degree-zero part the field \(\kappa(s)\). Applying the abstract graded
  Hilbert--Serre rationality lemma (\cref{lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational}) to the
  graded \(\kappa(s)\)-algebra \(R = R(X_s, L_s)\) and the finitely generated
  graded module \(M = M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s)\) exhibits the graded Hilbert
  function
  \(m \mapsto \dim_{\kappa(s)} \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\)
  (\cref{def:fiber_hilbert_function}) as a rational Hilbert function of some
  order \(d\) (\cref{def:ratHilb}): there are \(p \in \mathbb{Q}[X]\),
  \(d \in \mathbb{N}\) and an \(N\) with
  \(\dim_{\kappa(s)} \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})
  = [X^m]\,(p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d})\) for \(m > N\). The extraction lemma
  \cref{lem:hilbertPoly_of_isRatHilb} then yields a unique polynomial
  \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\) with
  \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}(m) = \dim_{\kappa(s)}
  \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\) for all \(m \gg 0\),

\end{proof}

\paragraph{Phase 1: Hilbert polynomial (\cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}).}
On a finite-type morphism \(\pi : X \to S\) with \(S\) noetherian and a
coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) on \(X\) with proper support over \(S\), the per-fiber
Hilbert polynomial is encoded as a function \(s \mapsto
\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\). Mathlib provides
\texttt{CategoryTheory.PolynomialModule} and Euler-characteristic
infrastructure on coherent sheaves on noetherian schemes; the polynomial
property of \(m \mapsto \chi(\mathcal{F} \otimes L^{\otimes m})\) requires
Snapper's Lemma, which is a project-side sub-build on top of the
graded-Euler-characteristic infrastructure.

\paragraph{Phase 1: Hilbert polynomial (\cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}).}
On a locally-of-finite-type morphism \(\pi : X \to S\) with \(S\) locally
noetherian, the per-fiber Hilbert polynomial is encoded as a total function
\(s \mapsto \Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\), formalized
sorry-free in \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/HilbertPolynomial.lean}
(\cref{def:fiber_module_restriction}, \cref{def:fiber_sections_module},
\cref{def:fiber_hilbert_function}, \cref{lem:hilbertPolynomial_unique},
\cref{lem:hilbertPoly_of_isRatHilb}). The \(H^0\)-only route through the
graded Hilbert function of the section graded module avoids Snapper's Lemma
and the Euler-characteristic infrastructure entirely; the outstanding
geometric input is the finite generation \cref{lem:sectionGradedModule_fg},
which feeds \cref{thm:hilbertPoly_of_sectionModule} through
\cref{lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational}.

text:
Base directory for this skill: /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.claude/skills/leandag

The DAG links blueprint nodes by `\uses` edges. An edge `source -> target` means
*target depends on source*. The engine records each node's `\lean` link,
`proved`/`leanok` status, effort metrics, and Lean source metadata.

## Keep It Fresh

- Run the project build after Lean/blueprint edits so generated Lean artifacts and
  imported libraries are current.
- Run `horizon blueprint` after blueprint or Lean declaration changes; it writes
  `.archon-horizon/blueprints/<project>.json` for the dashboard and cached DAG
  consumers.
- Use `horizon leandag` for live inspection when deciding what to prove next.

## `horizon leandag` (preferred)

- `horizon leandag` — per-project summary: nodes, edges, proved count, and any
  isolated nodes (no edge in or out — usually a blueprint bug to fix).
- `horizon leandag -p <project> --node <label>` — one node's direct deps, full
  transitive cone, and reverse deps.
- `horizon leandag -p a -p b --union` — merge projects by declaration/node id.
  Same-id nodes are combined; divergent non-signature metadata is tolerated, but
  a Lean signature mismatch is an error to investigate.
- `horizon leandag -p a -p b --intersect` — only shared declaration/node ids,
  with the same signature check.
- Add `--json` for machine-readable output.

A node is "ready" when all dependencies are proved. Prefer ready nodes when
choosing what to attempt next.

## Cached JSON

`horizon blueprint` writes each project's DAG to
`.archon-horizon/blueprints/<project>.json` (`nodes`, `edges`, `dangling`) for
the dashboard. Under the leandag engine, `\uses` are resolved at build/cache
time, so `dangling` is empty there; the parser fallback still populates it. To
check for orphan/unresolved nodes, use `horizon leandag` rather than relying only
on `dangling`.

data.content:
\section{Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf}
\label{sec:hilbert_polynomial}

\begin{definition}\leanok
[Hilbert polynomial]
  \label{def:hilbert_polynomial}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomial}
  \uses{def:fiber_hilbert_function, lem:hilbertPolynomial_unique}
  \source{nitsure-hilbert-quot}
  % ENCODING. Formalized sorry-free in
  % AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/HilbertPolynomial.lean as a TOTAL definition on
  % the graded Hilbert function hilbertFunction π L F s
  % (def:fiber_hilbert_function): if some polynomial Φ ∈ ℚ[λ] agrees with the
  % graded Hilbert function at all m > N for some N, then
  % hilbertPolynomial π L F s is such a Φ, produced by classical choice --
  % canonical because uniqueness holds UNCONDITIONALLY
  % (lem:hilbertPolynomial_unique) -- and otherwise the junk value 0. The
  % definition carries no properness/ampleness hypotheses; the geometric
  % regime (proper X_s, ample L_s, coherent F_s) enters only through the
  % existence statement thm:hilbertPoly_of_sectionModule and its
  % IsRatHilb-conditional form lem:hilbertPoly_of_isRatHilb. The construction
  % uses only H^0; for m≫0 it agrees with the Euler characteristic χ by Serre
  % vanishing, so it is the classical Hilbert polynomial and no numerical
  % invariant is lost.
  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
  % Polynomials" (read from
  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L453-L478).
  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5); cf.\ [Hartshorne],
  III.5.2.}
  Let \(X \to S\) be a finite-type morphism of noetherian schemes and let \(L\)
  be a line bundle on \(X\). For a coherent sheaf \(\mathcal{F}\) on \(X\) whose
  schematic support is proper over \(S\) and a point \(s \in S\), write \(X_s\)
  for the fibre over \(s\), \(\mathcal{F}_s = \mathcal{F}|_{X_s}\) and
  \(L_s = L|_{X_s}\). The \emph{Hilbert polynomial of \(\mathcal{F}\) at \(s\)
  relative to \(L\)} is the unique polynomial
  \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\) that agrees, for all
  \(m \gg 0\), with the graded Hilbert function
  (\cref{def:fiber_hilbert_function})
  \[
    m \;\longmapsto\; \dim_{\kappa(s)} \Gamma\bigl(X_s,\, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}\bigr)
  \]
  of the section module
  \(\bigoplus_{m \ge 0} \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\)
  (\cref{def:sectionGradedModule}), viewed as a finitely generated graded module
  over the homogeneous section ring
  \(\bigoplus_{m \ge 0} \Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m})\)
  (\cref{def:sectionGradedRing}). Uniqueness of \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}\) is
  unconditional (\cref{lem:hilbertPolynomial_unique}: two polynomials agreeing
  at all sufficiently large integers coincide); existence, for \(X_s\) proper
  over \(\kappa(s)\), \(L_s\) ample and \(\mathcal{F}_s\) coherent, is the
  content of \cref{thm:hilbertPoly_of_sectionModule}.

  This is the classical Hilbert polynomial. When \(\mathcal{F}_s\) is coherent
  and \(L_s\) is ample on the proper \(\kappa(s)\)-scheme \(X_s\), Serre vanishing
  gives \(H^i(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}) = 0\) for all \(i > 0\)
  and \(m \gg 0\), so for \(m \gg 0\)
  \[
    \dim_{\kappa(s)} \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})
    \;=\; \chi(X_s,\, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})
    \;=\; \sum_{i \ge 0} (-1)^i \dim_{\kappa(s)} H^i(X_s,\, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}),
  \]
  and hence \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}\) coincides with the Euler-characteristic
  Hilbert polynomial of Snapper's Lemma -- no numerical invariant is lost. The
  construction above, however, uses only the global sections \(H^0\): the higher
  cohomology enters solely through the classical statement that the two functions
  agree for \(m \gg 0\), never through the definition of \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}\)
  itself. When \(\mathcal{F}\) is \(S\)-flat the function
  \(s \mapsto \Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}\) is locally constant on \(S\), so \(\Phi\) is
  well-defined on connected components.
\end{definition}

\section{Graded Hilbert polynomial}
\label{sec:graded_hilbert_polynomial}

This section gives the graded-Hilbert-function construction that underlies
\cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}. It first records how a fibre \(X_s\) of a
family \(\pi : X \to S\) carries the data the construction consumes: the
restrictions \(\mathcal{F}_s\) and \(L_s\)
(\cref{def:fiber_module_restriction}) and the \(\kappa(s)\)-vector-space
structure on their sections (\cref{def:fiber_sections_module}). Working over
the fibre \(X_s\) with its ample line bundle \(L_s\) and coherent sheaf
\(\mathcal{F}_s\), it builds the section graded ring
(\cref{def:sectionGradedRing}) and module (\cref{def:sectionGradedModule})
together with the graded Hilbert function
(\cref{def:fiber_hilbert_function}), records their finite generation
(\cref{lem:sectionGradedModule_fg}), and extracts the Hilbert polynomial
(\cref{thm:hilbertPoly_of_sectionModule}): the graded Hilbert--Serre
rationality of the Hilbert series (\cref{lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational})
combines with Mathlib's polynomial-extraction lemma
(\cref{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}) in \cref{lem:hilbertPoly_of_isRatHilb},
and the extracted polynomial is unique by
\cref{lem:hilbertPolynomial_unique}. The route uses only \(H^0\)
